title: The kindest stranger I ever met
summary: Tell the story of one specific encounter with a stranger who was kind.
topic: The kindest stranger I ever met
prompt: Write 150–250 words about one stranger who was kind to you. Set the scene, describe what they did, and explain how it changed your day. Use past simple and the superlative 'the kindest'.
hintStarters: It was a cold Tuesday in…
I did not even know her name, but…
sampleText: The kindest stranger I ever met was a woman in Tokyo. It was a cold Tuesday evening in February, and I had just arrived at Shinjuku station. The platform was full of tired people in dark coats. It was rush hour. I was carrying a heavy suitcase and a small paper bag, and I did not know which exit to take. My Japanese was very basic. I stopped near the ticket gate and looked at the signs. The signs had too many lines and too many colours. I felt close to tears. A small woman with a grey scarf walked past me. Then she stopped, turned around, and came back. She did not speak much English, and I did not speak much Japanese. She pointed at my paper bag, where the name of my hotel was printed. She nodded and said, "Come." Without saying anything else, she walked with me through the station, up two sets of stairs, and outside into the freezing cold. She stood under the rain with me and pointed at a small street on the other side of the road. She bowed once and walked back into the station. I did not even know her name. But on that night, she was the kindest person in a city of thirteen million.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Superlative 'the kindest'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'The kindest stranger I ever met' — Dùng superlative để chỉ một người ở mức cao nhất theo cảm nhận của người viết.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The kindest stranger I ever met' — the superlative singles out one person at the top of a quality.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was carrying a heavy suitcase' (past continuous, mô tả tình huống) và 'she stopped, turned around, and came back' (past simple, hành động ngắn).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was carrying a heavy suitcase' (past continuous for the ongoing situation) and 'she stopped, turned around, and came back' (past simple for short actions).

sampleTranslationVi: Người lạ tử tế nhất tôi từng gặp là một người phụ nữ ở Tokyo. Hôm đó là một tối thứ Ba lạnh giá tháng Hai, và tôi vừa đến ga Shinjuku. Sân ga đông những người mệt mỏi trong áo khoác sẫm màu. Giờ cao điểm. Tôi đang xách một chiếc vali nặng và một túi giấy nhỏ, và không biết phải đi cửa ra nào. Tiếng Nhật của tôi rất sơ. Tôi dừng gần cổng soát vé và nhìn các bảng chỉ dẫn. Các bảng có quá nhiều dòng và quá nhiều màu. Tôi gần như muốn khóc. Một người phụ nữ nhỏ nhắn quàng khăn xám đi ngang qua tôi. Rồi bà dừng lại, quay người và trở lại. Bà không nói được nhiều tiếng Anh, tôi cũng không nói được nhiều tiếng Nhật. Bà chỉ vào túi giấy của tôi, nơi có in tên khách sạn. Bà gật đầu và nói: "Đi." Không nói gì thêm, bà đi cùng tôi qua nhà ga, lên hai đợt cầu thang, ra ngoài cái lạnh cắt da. Bà đứng dưới mưa với tôi và chỉ vào một con phố nhỏ bên kia đường. Bà cúi đầu chào một cái và quay vào nhà ga. Tôi thậm chí không biết tên bà. Nhưng đêm đó, bà là người tử tế nhất trong một thành phố mười ba triệu dân.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the superlative correctly?
options: The most kindest stranger I ever met was a woman in Tokyo.
The more kind stranger I ever met was a woman in Tokyo.
Kindest the stranger I ever met was a woman in Tokyo.
The kindest stranger I ever met was a woman in Tokyo.
I was carrying a heavy suitcase and a small paper bag.
I am carrying a heavy suitcase and a small paper bag.
I carry a heavy suitcase and a small paper bag.
I have carried a heavy suitcase and a small paper bag.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous = was/were + -ing, fits a background action in a past story.

options: She not speak much English.
She did not speak much English.
She did not spoke much English.
She no speak much English.
mcQuestions.explanation: Negative past simple: did not + base verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sequence of past actions.
options: She stopped, turn around, and come back.
She is stopping, turning around, and coming back.
She stopped, turned around, and came back.
She has stopped, has turned around, and has came back.
mcQuestions.explanation: Three past simple verbs in a row for a clean action sequence.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple for finished actions.

options: She pointed in my paper bag.
She pointed on my paper bag.
She pointed for my paper bag.
She pointed at my paper bag.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Point at + object' is the standard collocation.

options: Without saying anything else, she walked with me.
Without say anything else, she walked with me.
Without to say anything else, she walked with me.
Without said anything else, she walked with me.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'bow'.
options: She bow once and walk back into the station.
She bowed once and walked back into the station.
She has bowed once and has walk back into the station.
She is bowing once and walking back into the station.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'bowed' + 'walked' keeps the sequence consistent.

options: I did not even know her name, so she was the kindest person.
I did not even know her name, because she was the kindest person.
I did not even know her name, but she was the kindest person.
I did not even know her name, while she was the kindest person.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' signals contrast between not knowing her and rating her highly.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence using past continuous for setting.
options: The platform is full of tired people in dark coats.
The platform has been full of tired people in dark coats.
The platform full of tired people in dark coats.
The platform was full of tired people in dark coats.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'was' fits the rest of the story.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep tense consistent with the story.

options: On that night, she was the kindest person in a city of thirteen million.
In conclusion, kindness is the most important value.
Throughout history, strangers have helped travellers.
It is important to note that kindness costs nothing.
mcQuestions.explanation: A specific image (one person in a huge city) beats a generic line.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid 'In conclusion', 'Throughout history', or 'It is important to note'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small acts of kindness from strangers sometimes feel bigger than help from friends? What's different about them?
